content.content: 1 ![Build Status](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/workflows/CI/badge.svg) 2 3 [![Powered by the ODK](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Powered%20by&message=ODK&color=blue&style=flat&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://github.com/INCATools/ontology-development-kit) 4 [![Powered by the ROBOT](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Powered%20by&message=ROBOT&color=green&style=flat)](http://robot.obolibrary.org/) 5 6 7 8 # Uberon - a multi-species anatomy ontology 9 10 This repository contains the ontology *source*, and is primarily of 11 relevance to Uberon editors/developers. 12 13 For an overview of the ontology, plus usage instructions on how to 14 download the compiled versions of the ontology, please visit 15 [uberon.org](http://obophenotype.github.io/uberon/). 16 17 ## A note on the taxonomic scope of Uberon 18 19 Uberon has a set of upper level terms that are universal. These are indicated with the [common_anatomy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/core#common_anatomy) tag. However, 20 Uberon editors will not take requests for terms from outside of the Metazoa. Plant anatomy term requests should be directed to the [PO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/po.html), fungal anatomy terms to [FAO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/FAO.html). 21 Uberon also gives preference to anatomy term requests for vertebrates over invertebrates. Wherever possible term requesters are encouraged to work directly with existing invertebrate anatomy ontologies to get the terms they need. 22 23 ## Content 24 25 This repository contains the editors version of the ontology 26 (src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo), and the material required to make a release. 27 28 ## GitHub Actions Triggers 29 30 To trigger an automated human readable diff, add the following tag to a comment in your pull request: #gogoeditdiff 31 32 ## Editors: 33 34 See the file [README-editors.txt](bridge/../README-editors.txt) 35 36 See the [Standard Operating Procedure for Uberon Curators](bridge/../docs/uberon-editor-sop.md). 37

file.content: ![Build Status](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/workflows/CI/badge.svg) [![Powered by the ODK](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Powered%20by&message=ODK&color=blue&style=flat&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://github.com/INCATools/ontology-development-kit) [![Powered by the ROBOT](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Powered%20by&message=ROBOT&color=green&style=flat)](http://robot.obolibrary.org/) # Uberon - a multi-species anatomy ontology This repository contains the ontology *source*, and is primarily of relevance to Uberon editors/developers. For an overview of the ontology, plus usage instructions on how to download the compiled versions of the ontology, please visit [uberon.org](http://obophenotype.github.io/uberon/). ## A note on the taxonomic scope of Uberon Uberon has a set of upper level terms that are universal. These are indicated with the [common_anatomy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/core#common_anatomy) tag. However, Uberon editors will not take requests for terms from outside of the Metazoa. Plant anatomy term requests should be directed to the [PO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/po.html), fungal anatomy terms to [FAO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/FAO.html). Uberon also gives preference to anatomy term requests for vertebrates over invertebrates. Wherever possible term requesters are encouraged to work directly with existing invertebrate anatomy ontologies to get the terms they need. ## Content This repository contains the editors version of the ontology (src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo), and the material required to make a release. ## GitHub Actions Triggers To trigger an automated human readable diff, add the following tag to a comment in your pull request: #gogoeditdiff ## Editors: See the file [README-editors.txt](bridge/../README-editors.txt) See the [Standard Operating Procedure for Uberon Curators](bridge/../docs/uberon-editor-sop.md).

For an overview of the ontology, plus usage instructions on how to download the compiled versions of the ontology, please visit [uberon.org](http://obophenotype.github.io/uberon/). ## A note on the taxonomic scope of Uberon Uberon has a set of upper level terms that are universal. These are indicated with the [common_anatomy](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/core#common_anatomy) tag. However, Uberon editors will not take requests for terms from outside of the Metazoa. Plant anatomy term requests should be directed to the [PO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/po.html), fungal anatomy terms to [FAO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/FAO.html). Uberon also gives preference to anatomy term requests for vertebrates over invertebrates. Wherever possible term requesters are encouraged to work directly with existing invertebrate anatomy ontologies to get the terms they need. ## Content This repository contains the editors version of the ontology (src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo), and the material required to make a release. ## GitHub Actions Triggers To trigger an automated human readable diff, add the following tag to a comment in your pull request: #gogoeditdiff ## Editors: See the file [README-editors.txt](bridge/../README-editors.txt) See the [Standard Operating Procedure for Uberon Curators](bridge/../docs/uberon-editor-sop.md).

EOF
)"

gh pr create --title "Add blank line to README.md (closes #3702)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'

Resolves #3702 — adds a blank line to `README.md` as requested.

## Changes

- Appended a blank line to the end of `README.md`.

## Test plan

- [x] Diff inspected; only whitespace appended at EOF.

- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/actions/runs/25069230831)
EOF
)"
